company_type: Science and Technology - Life Sciences and Diagnostics

supply_chain.dependencies: The Company purchases raw materials from a large number of sources around the world. For some components that require particular specifications or regulatory or other qualifications only a single supplier or a limited number of suppliers can readily provide such components.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chains can be disrupted by inflation, supplier capacity constraints, fluctuations in demand, decreased availability of key raw materials or commodities, legislative or regulatory changes, bankruptcy or exiting of the business for other reasons and external events such as natural disasters, pandemic health issues, war, terrorist actions and governmental actions. Sustained interruptions in the supply of, or increase in the cost of, key components, raw materials, other commodities and labor can result in production interruptions, delays, extended lead times and inefficiencies.

regulatory_risks: FDA regulations on medical devices and in vitro diagnostics
EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR) and In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (IVDR)
UK regulatory changes post-Brexit
Switzerland regulatory changes post-withdrawal from EU Mutual Recognition Agreement
HIPAA privacy and security regulations
Federal Anti-Kickback Statute violations
False Claims Act violations
Open Payments Act reporting requirements
PPACA (Affordable Care Act) changes and healthcare reform
PAMA (Protecting Access to Medicare Act) pricing program impacts
China volume-based procurement program and price reduction initiatives
GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) compliance
State privacy laws (California and others)
Export/import control and economic sanctions laws (ITAR, EAR, OFAC)
Anti-boycott laws
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act
Environmental, health and safety laws and regulations
OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS) Pillar 2 global minimum tax
Tax law changes and audits (IRS, Denmark, other jurisdictions)
Antitrust laws
Defense Production Act (DPA) requirements
Expanded FDA regulation of laboratory-developed tests
Off-label marketing restrictions
Medical device recall and safety alert requirements
Clinical trial requirements and regulatory approvals
Government contract compliance and termination risks
Data privacy and cybersecurity regulations
Artificial intelligence regulations
Sustainability and climate change disclosure requirements
Diversity practice regulations

sanction_risks: Russia sanctions due to Ukraine conflict
Economic sanctions administered by U.S. Department of Treasury OFAC
Trade tensions between China and other countries including the U.S.
Comprehensive sanctions on certain countries with limited business dealings
